data.title: New Era Energy & Digital names Nelson as Chairman and CEO
data.description:
New Era Energy & Digital (NUAI) said Wednesday it appointed Charlie Nelson, currently President and COO, as its new Chairman and CEO, effective immediately, as part of a broader leadership reshuffle at the data center developer.

Current Chairman and CEO E. Will Gray II will transition off the board and into the role of President of the Permian, where he will continue to support New Era's (NUAI) Permian Basin relationships.

The company also named Ted Warner as President and CFO and appointed him to its board and tapped José Rodriguez as COO after he held data center and infrastructure roles at Microsoft, TikTok, and Amazon Web Services.

data.title: Salesforce climbs after Guggenheim upgrades to Buy
data.description:
Shares of Salesforce (CRM) climbed about 4% premarket on Wednesday after Guggenheim upgraded the stock to Buy from Neutral with a $228 price target, noting that "AI will pressure CRM, but not kill it."

Analysts led by John DiFucci said they upgraded the shares because they believe the company's current valuation presents an attractive entry point for investors.

"Our view on CRM remains unchanged: despite being a leading AI marketer, neither product traction nor material revenue contribution is yet visible in our channel conversations or in the reported numbers. Shares have declined nearly 41% YTD on fears of an impending "SaaSpocalypse." While we do see AI as a significant risk and view CRM as a company likely to be negatively impacted by the advent of Agentic AI, we believe the Armageddon scenario currently priced into the stock is misaligned with reality," said DiFucci and his team.

The analysts added that more realistically, they underwrite a scenario in which the company struggles to grow much but does not decline much either, while the current stock price implies that it declines 5% into perpetuity.

"To be clear, this is not a call that CRM will be a beneficiary of AI, but we don’t believe it will decline as implied in the current valuation," said the analysts.

data.title: Micron secures long-term agreement with GM as SCAs become broader revenue strategy
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) has secured a long-term Strategic Customer Agreement with General Motors (GM) to supply the U.S. automaker with memory and storage platforms necessary for vehicle production, the companies announced today.

It stands as one of 16 such Strategic Customer Agreements, or SCAs, that Micron executives discussed during the company's third quarter fiscal 2026 conference call last week. Micron said SCAs now account for about 40% of its business, and eventually half of the business will be tied to SCAs.

"So, I'll start by saying that these strategic customer agreements, or SCAs, cannot be canceled," said Sumit Sadana, Micron's executive vice president and chief business officer, during the call. "Now there is no provision in this agreement to enable the customer or allow a customer to walk away from this agreement. These are designed to be take-or-pay agreements outside of automotive. Generally, these are 5-year agreements. There are annual volume commitments for each of those years. And the take-or-pay means that whether they want to purchase the bits or not, they are obligated to pay for the price times the volume."

Micron said the SCA with GM is enabled in part due to its domestic investments, including $2B towards an advanced DRAM manufacturing fab in Manassas, Va., which entered production this year.

"As demand for memory and storage continues to grow, we are investing to extend supply availability, expand capacity and align more closely with our customers to improve supply predictability across the automotive ecosystem," said Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra. "Our expanding manufacturing efforts in the United States are designed to enable GM to deliver both near-term products as well as secure U.S.-based supply to support next-generation platforms and innovation."

data.title: Forget Spirit, JPMorgan says Allegiant Travel has a winning model
data.description:
JPMorgan initiated coverage of Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT) on Wednesday with an Overweight rating.

Analyst Jamie Baker said the demise of the ultra-low-cost carrier model has been widely reported given their paucity of premium, international, and loyalty, which has driven a sustainable advantage for legacy carriers. Spirit's second bankruptcy filing in a 12-month span was called the most notable proof point. However, while many LCCs remain profit deficient, Allegiant (ALGT) is noted to have posted consistent operating margins competitive with, and at times topping, industry leaders. 

Baker and his team believe Allegiant (ALGT) has written the playbook for the next generation of LCCs,  built on lower frequency and off-peak utilization to underserved secondary markets. The firm also sees meaningful upside following the Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) acquisition.

JPMorgan assigned a price target of $156 to ALGT. "Given the above-peer margins/margin durability, growth, and clean balance sheet, we believe a premium to the legacy peer set, similar to pre-COVID, is warranted," wrote Baker.

Shares of Allegiant Travel (ALGT) were up 0.5% in premarket action to $118.21.

data.title: Enterprise Products Partners co-CEO Teague to retire in January
data.description:
Enterprise Products Partners (EPD) said Wednesday that co-CEO Jim Teague plans to retire effective January 4, and co-CEO Randy Fowler will assume Teague's responsibilities upon his departure.

Teague and Fowler have served as co-CEOs since January 2020 and joined the company in 1999; Teague and Fowler have been directors of Enterprise GP since 2010 and 2011, respectively.

Upon Teague's retirement, Enterprise Products' (EPD) general partner will expand the Office of the Chairman, which is a management oversight group that serves as a liaison between the general partner and senior management; the Office of the Chairman will be comprised of Randa Duncan, Richard Bachmann, Randy Fowler, Michael "Tug" Hanley, and R. Daniel Boss.

data.title: Utilities sector slips in Q2 as Vistra leads gainers, Constellation Energy lags
data.description:
The utilities sector (XLU) underperformed during the quarter, declining 0.96%, compared with a 13.16% gain for the S&P 500.

Utilities Sector Scorecard

  • Sector Return: -0.96%
  • Best Performer: Vistra (VST) (+11.06%)
  • Worst Performer: Constellation Energy (CEG) (-14.93%)

Top utility sector performers of the quarter

On the individual front, Vistra (VST) was the biggest winner during the quarter, gaining 11.06% as investors responded positively to its AI-driven power generation story.

Seeking Alpha analyst Louis Gerard noted that the rally was supported by Vistra's contracted baseload generation, including its 20-year power purchase agreements with Meta and Amazon Web Services, plans to restart the Perry nuclear plant, a Fitch upgrade to investment-grade status, and its $10B Helix Digital Infrastructure joint venture with Nvidia, KKR, and KIA, reinforcing its position as a preferred power provider for AI-focused data centers.

It was followed by Dominion Energy (D), which advanced 10.89% during the quarter, supported by improving investor sentiment toward regulated utilities.

However, Gerard argued that the dominant catalyst behind the move was NextEra Energy's (NEE) $66.8B all-stock acquisition of Dominion at an approximately 23% premium, with the stock largely re-rating toward the deal terms rather than broader sector sentiment. The analyst added that while the transaction still carries regulatory and execution timeline risks, the breakup fee provides some downside protection if the deal is not completed.

Pinnacle West Capital (PNW) also posted a gain of 7.42% over the period.

Here are the top five gainers of the quarter:

  • Vistra (VST): +11.06%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 3.21 out of 5
  • Dominion Energy (D): +10.89%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 3.07 out of 5
  • Pinnacle West Capital (PNW): +7.42%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 3.02 out of 5
  • Alliant Energy (LNT): +7.34%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 2.97 out of 5
  • Evergy (EVRG): +6.49%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 3.01 out of 5

Top utility sector detractors of the quarter 

On the other hand, Constellation Energy (CEG) was the biggest detractor during the quarter, falling 14.93% as investors weighed its premium valuation, with some analysts arguing that much of its long-term AI-driven growth potential was already reflected in the stock price. Concerns over revenue concentration in the PJM market and the capital efficiency of its recent Calpine acquisition also weighed on sentiment.

Gerard further highlighted that the company's follow-on equity offering, the planned 4.4-GW PJM asset divestiture to LS Power as a regulatory remedy, and delays surrounding the proposed restart of the Crane nuclear facility added to investor concerns during the quarter.

Atmos Energy (ATO) also posted a notable decline, falling 6.09% despite beating its fiscal second-quarter expectations and raising its EPS guidance.

Gerard concluded that the stock experienced a "sell-the-news" reaction as several analysts lowered their price targets, while the company's lack of meaningful AI and data-center exposure limited investor enthusiasm.

FirstEnergy (FE) also declined 5.39% during the period.

 Here are the top five detractors of the quarter:

  • Constellation Energy (CEG): -14.93%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 2.73 out of 5
  • Atmos Energy (ATO): -6.09%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 3.16 out of 5
  • FirstEnergy (FE): -5.39%, Quant rating: Buy with a score of 3.63 out of 5
  • Exelon (EXC): -4.62%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 3.14 out of 5
  • American Water Works (AWK): -4.54%, Quant rating: Hold with a score of 2.74 out of 5

data.title: Surf Air Mobility announces two financing deals to cut convertible debt by 64%
data.description:
• Surf Air Mobility (SRFM) announced on Wednesday two financing transactions aimed at improving liquidity, extending debt maturities, and reducing future shareholder dilution.
  • The company will replace its remaining $47M senior secured convertible note with two new instruments: a $17M convertible note due 2027 and a $30M non-convertible term note due 2028.
  • The refinancing will reduce convertible debt by 64%, lowering the balance from $74M to about $17M.
  • Monthly debt payments are expected to decline by up to 50%, from as much as $4M to up to $2M.
  • The new $30M non-convertible term note will not require principal or interest payments until January 2027, easing near-term cash obligations.
  • Separately, the company secured a $21.6M aircraft-backed loan to boost working capital and liquidity, with the first funding received on June 30 and the remaining $14M expected within 30 days.
  • The refinancing of the convertible debt is expected to close on or around July 1, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. bond market hits historic 71-month drawdown
data.description:
The U.S. bond market is currently experiencing its longest drawdown in history, stretching an unprecedented 71 months, Charlie Bilello, chief market strategist at Creative Planning, said on Wednesday.

The current downturn, which started in August 2020, is also the most severe slump on record by a big margin, recording a maximum drawdown of 17.2%, Bilello highlighted, citing data from the Bloomberg U.S. Aggregate Bond Index spanning from January 1976 to June 2026.

To put this historical anomaly into perspective, the second-longest bond market drawdown occurred between July 1980 and October 1981. That period lasted just 16 months and saw a maximum decline of 9.0%.

data.title: Check Point Software rises after Guggenheim upgrades to Buy
data.description:
Shares of Check Point Software (CHKP) rose about 3% premarket on Wednesday after Guggenheim upgraded the stock to Buy from Neutral with a $188 price target.

"Fundamentally, we believe CHKP shares are trading materially below the intrinsic value of its recurring revenue if it were run hyper efficiently, in addition to its single digit FCF multiple. We believe Check Point is taking the necessary steps to achieve its aspiration of double-digit top line growth, but this upgrade is not dependent on that. At current prices, CHKP shares trade at 4.3x EV/NTM Recurring Revenue and 8.0x EV/NTM FCF, which is a discount to a group of security peers that trade at 11.6x Recurring Revenue and 35x FCF," said analysts led by John DiFucci.

The analysts believe there is a reason the stock trades at these levels, as the company reported a disappointing first quarter after a go-to-market, or GTM, transition that the company expects will impact second quarter results as well, in addition to being a single-digit grower for some time.

That said, the analysts believe the bad news is already in the stock at these levels, which presents an interesting buying opportunity for investors.

"Lastly, Check Point announced a $2B expansion of its share repurchase authorization on May 11, which we believe implies that the company will continue to be opportunistic with share repurchases, especially if shares remain at these levels," said DiFucci and his team.
